title: Linking Verbs, Responses & Listening
subtitle: Grammar, developing conversations and three audio tracks

blocks.title: Linking Verbs, Responses & Listening
blocks.text: Read the short theory, complete the language tasks, then listen to the three tracks.

blocks.title: Linking verbs
blocks.text: Linking verbs connect a person or thing with a state, appearance, sound, smell or taste. Use an adjective after feel, look, sound, seem, smell, taste and be: I feel guilty. You look stressed. He sounded fed up. She seems down. The flowers smell nice. It tastes awful.

blocks.title: Remember
blocks.text: Use an adjective, not an adverb, after a linking verb: It tastes awful — not awfully.

blocks.title: 3. Complete the chat room messages

items.prompt: He … after the argument.
I … about what happened.
She must … about the news.
You … today.
She … with the result.
I … when everyone looked at me.
He … when he read the message.
They … about the course.
You … now.

blocks.instructions: Complete each gap with a linking verb and adjective. Put the verb in the correct form.

items.prompt: This soup …
I always … before an exam.
These flowers …
Your clothes …
The new course …

blocks.title: 4. Match the news with the responses

items.prompt: My laptop has been stolen.
options: Congratulations! You can give me a lift to work!
That’s great. When is the wedding?
I’m sorry. I’m sure he’ll find a new one soon.
What a pain! Have you lost all your work?
That’s a relief! I know how worried you were.
What a shame! I’ll send you some photos.

blocks.title: Your opinion before listening
blocks.text: Choose True or False. These predictions are saved for comparison and are not graded.

blocks.title: Round 1 — your predictions

items.prompt: Before the dinner, Mark felt nervous.
options: True
False

items.prompt: He was wearing clothes that were too formal.
options: True
False

items.prompt: He felt he wasn’t very good at making conversation.
options: True
False

items.prompt: Annie’s parents already knew a lot about him.
options: True
False

items.prompt: Annie’s parents hadn’t liked her previous boyfriends.
options: True
False

items.prompt: Annie had forgotten to warn Mark about clothes.
options: True
False

items.prompt: She didn’t mind that he told some jokes.
options: True
False

items.prompt: She was pleased that Mark had met her parents.
options: True
False

blocks.instructions: Do this before listening. There are no right or wrong answers in this round.

blocks.title: Three audio tracks
blocks.text: Listen first to Mark, then Mrs deVere and Annie. You can replay each track.

blocks.title: 1. Mark
blocks.text: Use this track for exercises 5 and 6.

blocks.title: 2. Mrs deVere
blocks.text: Listen for her opinion about Mark.

blocks.title: 3. Annie
blocks.text: Listen for Annie’s version of the event.

blocks.title: 5. What event is Mark talking about?

items.prompt: Choose one answer.
options: a wedding
meeting his girlfriend’s parents for the first time
